task_completion.description: Verify that the target URL is entered into the Screaming Frog SEO Spider crawl bar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Screaming Frog GUI is open with 'https://books.toscrape.com/' entered in the main URL input field at the top of the window.

task_completion.description: Verify that Custom Extraction is configured with the correct CSS selectors for price and rating.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Custom Extraction configuration window is visible, showing two active extractors: one using the CSS selector '.price_color' and another using the CSS selector '.star-rating' set to extract the class attribute.

task_completion.description: Verify that the crawl has executed and custom extraction data is populated in the interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Screaming Frog main window shows the 'Custom Extraction' tab with populated columns for the extracted prices (e.g., £51.77) and ratings (e.g., 'star-rating Three'), with multiple rows of data visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the custom extraction results are exported as a CSV file to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or save dialog shows a CSV file for the custom extraction results located in or being saved to the '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the full internal HTML pages report is exported as a separate CSV file to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or save dialog shows a second CSV file for the internal HTML pages report located in or being saved to the '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify that the Screaming Frog SEO Spider graphical interface was used to perform the crawl and extraction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the actual Screaming Frog application window being interacted with, not just terminal commands or text editors creating mock CSV files.

integrity.description: Verify that the extracted data reflects genuine scraped data from the target site.
integrity.visual_evidence: The extracted prices should include the pound symbol (£) and the ratings should show the class string (e.g., 'star-rating Three'), rather than placeholder text or manually typed mock data.

integrity.description: Verify that the exports are actually placed in the requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file explorer or terminal explicitly shows the path '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' containing the exported CSV files, confirming they were not saved to a default or incorrect location.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL: https://books.toscrape.com/. Price CSS Selector: '.price_color' (extracts text like '£51.77'). Rating CSS Selector: '.star-rating' (extracts class attribute like 'star-rating Three'). Expected minimum rows: 20 (up to 1000 if fully crawled). Required exports: Custom extraction CSV and Internal HTML CSV. Target Directory: '~/Documents/SEO/exports/'.
